title: The Truth Behind the Waiting
description: On pages 7–10, the truth behind the waiting as crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain.
scene: The Truth Behind the Waiting. Crocus explains why Laboon keeps hurting himself and reveals the promise that left the whale waiting at Reverse Mountain. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: It's about those pirates who entrusted you to me...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It seems they've already left Grand Line...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I know they were your good friends,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: And that you think they would've kept their promise no matter what...

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: But they're not going to come back here ever again.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Oooh! Bobh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Lab- con...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Voh! Both

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Laboon! Listen to what I'm saying!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: BO BHH! !!!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Those pirates have already-

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I said they've already L-

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Puooo Ohhh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Lab- Don!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I understand your feelings of denial! But you have to accept the truth...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Aghhh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Please listen to what I'm saying!

dialogue.speaker: Laboon
dialogue.text: Buoh 924!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Buqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Laboon!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: guoo ohhh!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: you...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Ever since then...

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Laboon has been bellowing at reverse mountain,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: and bashing his head against the red line,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: As if trying to stubbornly assert that his friends are beyond the wall,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: And that they'll return someday...!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I've tried telling the truth many more times since then,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But Laboon still refuses to accept the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: What a whale... He still keeps on waiting even after being betrayed...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Even though there's no point in waiting anymore...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's exactly it... it's because he'll lose all reason,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: that he refuses to listen.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: He's afraid of losing the meaning behind his waiting above all else.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: This whale's home is back in West Blue, which is located on the other side of Red Line.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It's too late for him to go back.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: That's why his only hope,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: are the very friends he came along with here in the first place.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...but you know, as much as I feel sorry for this whale and all,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: You've also been betrayed by them if you think about it.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: So why don't you just leave the whale be already?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Look at the scars on his head...

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the truth behind the waiting. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
